premise: When my widowed mother remarried, I moved in at 55 and poured $70,000 of my savings into a down-to-the-studs renovation after her new husband promised I’d inherit her half of the $390,000 house; the day after her funeral he produced a backdated forged will leaving everything to his new girlfriend and evicted me. I hired a forensic handwriting expert, proved the will was a fabrication, and a judge awarded me the entire home — I now run it as a rent-free transitional shelter for women fleeing domestic abuse, with a brass plaque on the front door that reads ‘Never Trust a Backdated Signature.’

thumb.detail: My Stepfather Promised Me Half The House, Then Forged A Will. So I Turned It Int
